mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad economic strength, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its cyclical low signals a potential recession, increasing risk aversion and causing a flight to safety away from equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances in the 20 trading days following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) is already elevated, it signals the Federal Reserve is behind the curve, forcing more aggressive future tightening which contracts multiples and hurts equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and FEDFUNDS level fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient in a walk-forward regression against 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a healthy real economy response to stimulus, boosting earnings growth and equity valuations. This effect is muted or reversed during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO momentum and S&P 500 returns is not significantly more positive during easing cycles compared to tightening cycles (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) indicates stronger-than-expected consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in near-term revenue forecasts and a positive short-term market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:Market reaction to economic data releases
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the risk-free rate, making bonds and cash more attractive relative to equities. This raises the discount rate on future earnings, compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: There is no statistically significant negative correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns when the rate is above the trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer confidence and investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate profits and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to underperform its long-term average in the 60 days following a trigger event in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth is bullish for equities only when inflation is contained. When high growth is accompanied by high inflation, it signals an overheating economy, prompting monetary tightening that outweighs the benefits of growth and hurts returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY change > 3% AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3%
failure_condition: The performance of the S&P 500 during periods of high growth and high inflation is not statistically worse than during periods of high growth and low inflation.
